sections.instruction: Complete the notes below. Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
sections.passage_or_context:
Noise in Cities
Past research focused on noise level (measured in decibels) and people’s responses.
Noise ‘maps’
• Show that the highest noise levels are usually found on roads
• Do not show other sources of noise e.g. when windows are open or people’s neighbours are in their (31) {{BLANK}}
• Ignore variations in people’s perceptions of noise
• Have made people realize that the noise is a (32) {{BLANK}} issue that must be dealt with
Problems caused by noise
• Sleep disturbance
• Increase in amount of stress
• Effect on the (33) {{BLANK}} of school children
Different types of noise
Some noises can be considered pleasant e.g. the sound of a (34) {{BLANK}} in a town
To investigate this, researchers may use methods from (35) {{BLANK}} sciences
What people want
Plenty of activity in urban environments which are (36) {{BLANK}} but also allow people to relax
But architects and town planners
• Do not get much (37) {{BLANK}} in acoustics
• Regards sound as the responsibility of engineers
Understanding sound as an art form
We need to know
• How sound relates to (38) {{BLANK}}
• What can be learnt from psychology about the effects of sound
• Whether physics can help us understand the (39) {{BLANK}} of sound
Virtual reality programs
• Advantage: predict the effect of buildings
• Current disadvantage: they are (40) {{BLANK}}
